column1: #  DCGM exporter — custom metrics list
#  Trimmed from the upstream default (~80 fields) to the dozen we actually
#  use in the sandbox dashboards. Format is positional:
#      DCGM_FI_<field>
#  Lines starting with `#` are comments. Whitespace is significant only as a
#  separator. The exporter prefixes everything with `DCGM_FI_` automatically
#  but we include it explicitly to make grepping easier.
#  Field categories:
#    DEV_*    — basic device telemetry (power
#    PROF_*   — Data Center Profiling. Higher accuracy than DEV_GPU_UTIL but
#               requires the DCGM profiling subsystem to be active. On
#               consumer cards (4060) some PROF_* may report N/A; that's
#               fine — we just don't plot them.
#  If a value never appears in /metrics output
#  this GPU or DCGM hasn't finished initialising. The dcgm/README.md
#  troubleshooting section lists the usual suspects.
DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE
DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP
column3: GPU die temperature in degrees Celsius

column1: DCGM_FI_DEV_MEMORY_TEMP
column3: GPU memory temperature in degrees Celsius (may be N/A on consumer cards)

column1: DCGM_FI_DEV_SM_CLOCK
DCGM_FI_DEV_MEM_CLOCK
# On an 8 GB 4060: USED + FREE should sum to ~8188 MiB.
DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED
DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE
# DEV_GPU_UTIL is the nvidia-smi-style "GPU-Util". It only measures whether
# *any* kernel was running
column2: not how saturated the SMs are. Misleading for

column1: # LLM inference — keep it visible so you can see *why* it's misleading.
DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL
column3: Coarse GPU utilisation (nvidia-smi style; misleading for LLMs)

column1: # --- DCP profiling metrics — UNAVAILABLE ON CONSUMER GPUs ----------------
# The DCGM Data Center Profiling (DCP) module exposes the metrics below.
# These are the *honest* saturation numbers — much better than DEV_GPU_UTIL.
# BUT: the DCP module is gated to data-centre cards (H100
# On consumer Ada cards like the RTX 4060 the exporter logs:
#     "Not collecting DCP metrics: This request is serviced by a module
#      of DCGM that is not currently loaded"
# and silently skips these fields. Left here
# the same compose on a data-centre GPU can uncomment them and immediately
# get the better saturation panel.
# DCGM_FI_PROF_SM_ACTIVE
column3: Fraction of time at least one warp is active on an SM (real GPU util)

column1: # DCGM_FI_PROF_SM_OCCUPANCY
column3: Fraction of resident warps over the SM maximum

column1: # DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE
column3: Fraction of time the tensor-core pipe is busy

column1: # DCGM_FI_PROF_DRAM_ACTIVE
column3: Fraction of memory bandwidth in use
